title: Subitems of Subitems
excerpt: It would be great if there was a way to do sub items of subitems.  Kind of like how JIRA or ADO do User Stories then Tasks or bugs, etc.  So to be able to drill down more below sub items.

posts.author: HarveyHeise (Harvey Heise)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great if there was a way to do sub items of subitems. Kind of like how JIRA or ADO do User Stories then Tasks or bugs, etc. So to be able to drill down more below sub items.

posts.author: timyshark (Timy Shark)
posts.cookedHtml: It depends on how granular you like to go, I use linked boards to do that sub of sub …for as many as you like, probably neater to have sub-items separated in another board?

posts.author: EMorton (Erik Morton)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a feature info board, that I actually wish had this added level of granularity as well. We’ve currently gotten around it by heading in the opposite direction, similar to what @timyshark suggested) and essentially using Groups as Feature Set level, then Items as Features themselves, and then subitems as the Aspects of those Features. That said, it has ballooned my Group count, so it’s not a perfect setup.

posts.author: BenSchneider (Benjamin Schneider)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does anybody care about this feature? I´m thinking about to move everything to clickup, just because of the missing sub-subitem feature in monday…

for years people are requesting this feature here in the forum, but nobody cares…
